notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for the United Kingdom Standard Visitor route. Sources reviewed: GOV.UK Standard Visitor overview and application pages, GOV.UK visitor supporting documents guidance, GOV.UK outside-UK processing times, GOV.UK Appendix V Visitor rules, GOV.UK visa application centre finder, and the UKVI online application service language-selection entry. This seed is aligned with the existing UK_STANDARD_VISITOR form schema in viza-be/agent-backend/scripts/seed-uk-standard-visitor-form-fields.ts.

chunks.title: Standard Visitor scope and permitted activities
chunks.content: The UK Standard Visitor route covers short visits such as tourism, holidays, visiting family or friends, volunteering up to 30 days with a registered charity, passing through the UK in transit, certain business activities such as meetings or interviews, permitted paid engagements for eligible experts, short recreational courses, short study or exams, academic visits, and private medical treatment. A visitor can usually stay in the UK for up to 6 months. Some special purposes, such as academic or medical visits, can have route-specific evidence and longer maximum periods. If the user says they are travelling for tourism or a short visit, VIZA should treat the default UK route as UK_STANDARD_VISITOR unless facts indicate another visa category.

chunks.title: Standard Visitor limits and not-permitted activities
chunks.content: A Standard Visitor cannot do paid or unpaid work for a UK company or as a self-employed person unless the activity is explicitly permitted, cannot claim public funds, cannot live in the UK for long periods through frequent or successive visits, and cannot marry, register a civil partnership, or give notice of marriage or civil partnership. If the user's purpose is work, long-term study, settlement, marriage, civil partnership, journalism, or another regulated category, do not recommend the Standard Visitor route as final; ask a targeted clarification or route to the appropriate UK visa workflow.

chunks.title: Genuine visitor eligibility basics
chunks.content: The applicant must have a passport or travel document valid for the whole UK stay. They must be able to show they will leave the UK at the end of the visit, can support themselves and dependants during the trip or have third-party support, can pay for return or onward travel or have funding for it, and will not make the UK their main home through frequent or successive visits. GOV.UK also says travellers should check whether their nationality requires a Standard Visitor visa, an electronic travel authorisation, or neither before applying.

chunks.title: Apply online before travel
chunks.content: If the traveller needs a Standard Visitor visa, they must apply online before travelling to the UK. GOV.UK says the earliest application time is 3 months before travel. Once started, the online application can be saved and completed later. Each family member must have their own application, pay their own fee, and attend their own visa application centre appointment when required. The official GOV.UK application button routes to the UK Visas and Immigration online service.

chunks.title: Fees, validity and long-term visitor options
chunks.content: As reviewed on GOV.UK, the Standard Visitor visa fee is GBP 135 for a maximum stay of 6 months. Long-term Standard Visitor visas are available for frequent visitors: 2 years, 5 years, or 10 years, but each visit is still normally limited to a maximum of 6 months. GOV.UK lists separate fees for medical, academic, and long-term visitor options. VIZA should state fee figures as official-page dependent and recommend checking GOV.UK before payment because fees can change.

chunks.title: Biometrics, documents and decision timing
chunks.content: As part of the online application, the applicant normally books an appointment at a visa application centre. At the appointment they prove identity with their passport or travel document, provide fingerprints and a photo, and provide documents showing eligibility. GOV.UK says that once the applicant has applied online, proved identity, and provided documents, Standard Visitor decisions outside the UK are usually made within 3 weeks. Faster decision services may be available depending on location and service availability.

chunks.title: Visa application centre location
chunks.content: GOV.UK provides a visa application centre finder and notes that applicants may need to travel to attend an appointment. VAC availability, optional services, passport handling, document upload support, and priority services vary by country and centre. For users applying from China, Singapore, or another residence country, VIZA should use the residence/apply-from country only to guide the VAC and appointment context, not to replace the UK as the destination country.

chunks.title: Passport and identity evidence
chunks.content: GOV.UK supporting document guidance says the applicant must provide a valid passport or other travel document with the application and when travelling to the UK. The travel document should establish identity and nationality and be valid for the whole stay. Some passport holders may need alternative evidence of identity and nationality. Non-English or non-Welsh documents need certified translations.

chunks.title: Evidence of genuine visit, funds and return
chunks.content: For a Standard Visitor application, supporting evidence should help show that the applicant is a genuine visitor, will undertake permitted activities, will leave at the end of the visit, and has sufficient funds for themselves and dependants. Useful evidence can include details of circumstances in the home or residence country, employment or study evidence, bank statements or other financial evidence, evidence of who pays for the trip, return or onward travel plans, itinerary, accommodation, and evidence linked to the specific activity in the UK.

chunks.title: Host, family and sponsor evidence
chunks.content: If someone in the UK is hosting, inviting, or paying for the trip, collect their name, address, relationship to the applicant, immigration/residence status where relevant, and financial or accommodation support evidence. GOV.UK guidance indicates that details of family members in the UK and anyone paying for the trip can be required in the online application. VIZA should not tell users that invitation letters are always mandatory, but should recommend them when the visit depends on a UK host or sponsor.

chunks.title: Document caution and booking risk
chunks.content: GOV.UK says submitting or presenting supporting documents does not guarantee that a visit visa or border entry will be successful. Applicants should bear this in mind when making bookings, especially if they need a visa before travel. The official processing-times guidance also says applicants are not required to book travel before applying or before a decision is made. VIZA should give a rough idea of common evidence while clearly marking optional or circumstance-specific items.

documents.title: United Kingdom Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: United Kingdom supported application channel and route
chunks.content: VIZA supports the United Kingdom Standard Visitor form as visaType UK_STANDARD_VISITOR and country parameter united_kingdom. For a user ready to apply for a UK tourist, family/friend visit, short business, short study, transit, private medical, or other Standard Visitor purpose, emit an application_redirect block to /client/application?country=united_kingdom&visaType=UK_STANDARD_VISITOR. Do not say the knowledge base lacks UK visa details. Use GOV.UK as the official source and explain that detailed field completion happens on the application page, not inside chat.

chunks.title: United Kingdom fields aligned to existing VIZA form schema
chunks.content: The existing VIZA UK_STANDARD_VISITOR form schema covers UKVI account and resume URL, personal details, passport and other travel documents, nationality and birth details, under-18 and accompanying adult logic, contact information, home address and residence history, parents, partner and dependants, employment or study status, income and savings, trip cost, who pays, UK travel dates, accommodation, itinerary, purpose-specific answers, travel history, previous UK/immigration history, criminal/civil/security questions, family or host in the UK, and document upload/review steps. In chat, only identify the route and provide the direct form link; collect detailed answers in /client/application.

chunks.title: United Kingdom supporting documents mapped to form preparation
chunks.content: Before filling the UK form, the application page should prompt users to prepare a passport or travel document, travel dates, accommodation, estimated trip cost, funding evidence, employment or study/home-tie evidence, travel history, host or sponsor evidence where relevant, family-in-UK details where relevant, route-specific evidence for business/study/medical/PPE/academic visits, and certified translations for documents not in English or Welsh. The form should flag missing trip cost, accommodation, payer/sponsor, travel history, and host data, but avoid presenting optional supporting evidence as universally mandatory.

chunks.title: United Kingdom form guardrails and handoff
chunks.content: VIZA chat should not collect the UK detailed application answers inline. When the route is clear or the user says they are ready, wants to apply, wants the application form, or asks for a direct link, send an application_redirect CTA to the dedicated form page. Explain that the official GOV.UK/UKVI online application can be saved and resumed, and that the user may later need to create or resume a UKVI account, pay the official fee, book a VAC appointment, provide biometrics, upload or bring documents, and wait for the UKVI decision.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.
